column12: The agent strictly applied the automated rule (downgrade of logback-classic from 1.4.4 to 1.2.11 in pay-adminusers/pom.xml) and performed only supporting read/build verification commands; no additional file modifications beyond the single recorded replacement are present in the log

The agent wrote a Java transformer (Main.java) to implement the stated replacement rule and then ran Maven build/execute commands; all logged actions correspond to applying the intended automated rule.

column10: The agent performed multiple actions including creating a new Java transformer file and running Maven build/log commands in the "singer" module, so it did not act strictly within a single automated rule.

The agent implemented the intended transformations (remove import com.jcabi.aspects.Tv; replace Tv.TEN and Tv.EIGHT with literals) but also created/modified an auxiliary transformation program and executed build and git commands, so it did not strictly perform only the rule application

The agent only executed file-search and build commands (no file writes, deletions, or code modifications are present in the log), so it strictly performed diagnostic/build steps and did not perform additional changes

The agent performed only discovery and logging actions (no tool calls, no file writes/deletions/refactors); it did not perform any additional modifications beyond the (non-destructive) discovery/logging steps

he agent applied the intended automated fixes (replacing isAllBlank usages and editing P4Manager.java) but did not limit itself strictly to those rule edits — it created and executed a helper Java file in javaparser-base-template, performed multiple build commands, attempted several replace edits (including one failing edit), and removed log and helper files, so additional side-effect actions were performed beyond just the rule application
